title: Project Overview
description: Start here to understand the core mission of FarmGame: a 2D tile-based farming simulator. The README provides the high-level context and inspiration for the project.

title: Main Game Loop
description: The Game class is the heart of the engine. It manages the lifecycle of the application, including initialization, the update-render loop, and high-level state management.

title: Graphics & Tile Rendering
description: Learn how pixels are drawn to the screen. The Screen class handles buffer management, while the rendering guide explains how sprites are extracted from sheets and displayed.
languageLesson: Direct pixel manipulation in Java allows for high-performance 2D rendering by bypassing high-level graphics APIs for core drawing operations.

title: The Simulation Brain
description: The FarmGrid manages the 2D world. It orchestrates daily updates, weather changes, and coordinates interactions between individual cells in the farming grid.

title: Crop Lifecycle & Entities
description: The Crop class defines the blueprint for all plants. Concrete implementations like Wheat and Tomato specify unique growth rates, values, and visual stages.
languageLesson: Abstract classes and inheritance are used here to share common plant logic while allowing specific crops to override behavior like harvesting and growth requirements.

title: Dynamic Weather System
description: Weather effects directly impact the simulation. The Weather interface allows for diverse conditions like Rain or Heat Waves to be plugged in, altering soil moisture and growth rates.
languageLesson: The Strategy pattern is implemented here via the Weather interface, allowing the simulation to change its behavior at runtime based on the active weather condition.

title: Player Input & Interaction
description: Input handlers translate user actions into game commands. Tools define how players interact with the farm, from watering crops to harvesting mature plants.

title: Project Build System
description: Finally, see how the project is put together. The Maven pom.xml manages the dependencies and build plugins required to compile and run the Java application.
